title: European Union Young Professional Experts Programme 2026: Apply for International Career Opportunities in Georgia and Armenia
summary: The European External Action Service (EEAS) has officially opened applications for the Young Professional Experts (YPE) Programme 1-2026, offering an exceptional opportunity for young professionals from European Union Member States to contribute to international peace, security, governance, and cooperation efforts through European Union missions abroad. This prestigious programme provides selected candidates with the opportunity to work within two significant European Union Common Security and Defence Policy (CSDP) missions: the European Union Monitoring Mission in Georgia (EUMM Georgia) and the European Union Mission in Armenia (EUM Armenia). The initiative is designed to attract talented young professionals seeking hands-on international experience while supporting the European Union’s efforts to promote stability, security, and conflict prevention in strategically important regions. What is the Young Professional Experts Programme? The Young Professional Experts Programme is a specialized European Union initiative aimed at developing the next generation of international affairs professionals. Through placements in EU missions abroad, participants gain practical experience in multinational environments while working alongside experienced experts and diplomats. The programme serves as a valuable platform for career development, providing participants with exposure to international cooperation, mission operations, policy implementation, digital innovation, governance systems, and peacebuilding activities. Successful candidates are integrated into professional teams where they contribute directly to mission objectives and daily operations. Available Opportunities For the 2026 intake, positions are available within: European Union Monitoring Mission in Georgia (EUMM Georgia) The European Union Monitoring Mission in Georgia is one of the EU’s flagship civilian missions. It contributes to stability and confidence-building efforts in Georgia by monitoring developments on the ground and supporting peace and security initiatives. One of the advertised positions under the Young Professional Experts Programme includes a role focused on Digital Collaboration and Innovation, providing successful applicants with an opportunity to contribute to modern information management and digital transformation initiatives within the mission framework. European Union Mission in Armenia (EUM Armenia) The European Union Mission in Armenia supports peace and stability efforts within the region while promoting confidence-building measures and strengthening security cooperation. Young professionals selected for placements within EUM Armenia will have the opportunity to gain practical international experience while supporting mission activities and objectives. Why This Opportunity Matters The Young Professional Experts Programme offers significantly more than traditional employment opportunities. Participants become part of a wider European effort aimed at fostering peace, security, and international cooperation. Key benefits include: Professional experience within European Union international missions. Exposure to multinational and multicultural work environments. Networking opportunities with diplomats, policymakers, security experts, and international professionals. Practical experience in conflict prevention, monitoring, governance, and peacebuilding. Development of leadership, analytical, communication, and project management skills. Enhanced career prospects within international organizations, governmental institutions, and development agencies. Opportunity to contribute directly to European Union external action initiatives. Eligibility Requirements The programme is specifically targeted at nationals of European Union Member States. Candidates must apply through the established EEAS recruitment mechanisms and must be included in the list of seconded candidates maintained by their respective national authorities. Applicants who are not already registered within the EEAS recruitment platform are required to create the necessary profiles and obtain the appropriate login credentials before submitting their applications. The positions are accessible only through the secondment process coordinated by national authorities. Application Process Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official Goalkeeper recruitment system managed by the European External Action Service. Candidates who already possess an EU Login and Goalkeeper account may proceed directly with their applications. Those who do not yet have access must first complete the registration process before applying. Because these positions operate under a secondment framework, prospective applicants are encouraged to contact their national authorities early in the process to ensure they are included among eligible seconded candidates. Important Deadline Applications for the Young Professional Experts Programme 1-2026 must be submitted no later than: 07 July 2026 at 17:00 (Brussels Time). Given the competitive nature of European Union mission placements, interested candidates are encouraged to begin preparations as early as possible. Final Thoughts The European Union Young Professional Experts Programme 2026 represents a unique gateway into international public service. Whether your interests lie in peacebuilding, international cooperation, digital innovation, governance, or security policy, this programme provides a rare opportunity to gain firsthand experience within European Union missions operating on the front lines of regional stability and cooperation. For ambitious young professionals seeking to build impactful international careers, the Young Professional Experts Programme offers a strong foundation, practical experience, and exposure to some of the most important diplomatic and security initiatives undertaken by the European Union today. Deadline : 07 July 2026 at 17:00 (Brussels Time). APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here. The post European Union Young Professional Experts Programme 2026: Apply for International Career Opportunities in Georgia and Armenia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU Project Officer Position at Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) Brussels: Advance International Development, Democracy, and Social Justice Through EU-Funded Projects
summary: Professionals seeking to build a meaningful career in international development, European Union project management, democracy promotion, and global cooperation now have an exciting opportunity to join one of Germany’s most respected political foundations. The Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) Brussels Office is currently recruiting an EU Project Officer to strengthen its dynamic EU-funded projects team. This position offers an exceptional opportunity to work at the intersection of international development cooperation, European Union external action, civil society strengthening, and project development. Successful candidates will contribute to the design, preparation, and coordination of EU-funded initiatives that support social justice, democratic governance, and civic engagement across numerous countries. About the opportunity. The Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) is Germany’s oldest political foundation and is committed to the principles and values of social democracy, including freedom, justice, solidarity, and democratic participation. Operating in more than 100 countries worldwide, FES collaborates with a broad network of partners to promote sustainable development, democratic governance, social inclusion, and international cooperation. The Brussels-based EU external funding team plays a crucial role in supporting FES offices across the globe. The team helps conceptualize, develop, and secure EU-funded projects that contribute to development cooperation and strengthen civil society institutions. Through partnerships with civil society organizations, local authorities, trade unions, think tanks, research institutions, and media organizations, FES supports impactful initiatives that create positive change in communities worldwide. Overview of the EU Project Officer Role The EU Project Officer will join a young and dynamic team responsible for identifying funding opportunities and supporting the preparation and submission of high-quality project proposals for European Union funding programs. The role combines administrative, analytical, strategic, and creative responsibilities. It is ideal for professionals who enjoy project development, international collaboration, proposal writing, stakeholder engagement, and working in multicultural environments. The selected candidate will work closely with FES country offices, international partners, consultants, and EU institutions to develop competitive funding proposals and strengthen organizational capacity in project management. Key Responsibilities The successful candidate will undertake a wide range of responsibilities, including: Monitoring EU Funding Opportunities The Project Officer will regularly review and analyze calls for proposals and funding opportunities related to: Development cooperation Democracy promotion EU enlargement policy Neighborhood policy Political education Civil society development International cooperation This involves identifying funding opportunities that align with FES strategic priorities and partner needs. Project Development and Proposal Writing A significant component of the role involves: Designing project concepts Drafting project proposals Coordinating proposal submissions Developing logical frameworks and project structures Collaborating with FES offices and partner organizations worldwide Ensuring compliance with EU funding requirements The officer will contribute both technically and administratively to the proposal development process. Coordination of External Experts The role requires managing relationships with external service providers, including: Consultants Technical experts Translators Subject-matter specialists These experts often contribute to proposal development and project implementation activities. Supporting Ongoing EU Projects Although implementation primarily takes place in partner countries, the Project Officer will provide support to ongoing projects by: Responding to project-related inquiries Reviewing reports Providing technical guidance Assisting with compliance and documentation requirements Knowledge Management The successful candidate will contribute to institutional learning by helping maintain and improve organization-wide knowledge on EU-funded projects. This includes documenting best practices, sharing lessons learned, and supporting internal knowledge-sharing initiatives. Training and Capacity Building The officer will participate in: Internal FES training programs Workshops on EU funding mechanisms Project kick-off meetings Capacity-building initiatives for staff and partners Engagement with EU Institutions The position also involves attending relevant: EU consultations Conferences Networking events Stakeholder meetings Policy discussions This exposure provides valuable opportunities to build professional networks and stay informed about emerging EU priorities. Benefits of the Position FES offers a highly attractive professional environment that includes: A fixed-term employment contract initially lasting twelve months Salary based on professional experience Extensive professional development opportunities Exposure to international development projects Collaboration with partners in more than 100 countries A supportive and inclusive workplace culture Flexible working arrangements that support work-life balance Meaningful work with measurable impact in partner countries Opportunities to contribute to social justice and democratic development globally Preferred Qualifications and Experience FES welcomes applicants who possess experience in areas such as: EU external assistance programs EuropeAid initiatives NDICI-GE programs IPA projects Horizon Europe projects Erasmus+ projects CERV (Citizens, Equality, Rights and Values) projects International development cooperation Political foundations Civil society programming Project proposal development and management Additional language skills in French, Spanish, or Portuguese are considered advantageous. Why You Should Apply This position presents a unique opportunity for professionals passionate about international development, democracy promotion, social justice, and European cooperation. Working with FES means contributing to impactful projects that strengthen institutions, empower communities, and promote democratic values across the globe. Candidates seeking meaningful international careers, extensive networking opportunities, and exposure to EU-funded development initiatives should strongly consider applying before the deadline. Deadline:22 June,2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this information, click here. For more global opportunities, click here. The post EU Project Officer Position at Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) Brussels: Advance International Development, Democracy, and Social Justice Through EU-Funded Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Code for Africa Civic Graph Fellowship 2026 in South Africa: Fully Supported Opportunity to Build Open Civic Data and Strengthen Information Integrity
summary: The Code for Africa (CfA) Civic Graph Fellowship 2026 is now accepting applications from aspiring civic technologists, analysts, researchers, and university students in South Africa who are passionate about open data, democracy, and information integrity. Through its Open Data Initiative, Code for Africa is offering seven part-time Civic Graph Fellowships aimed at strengthening civic data ecosystems and supporting the development of standardized, open-source civic information across South Africa. The fellowship presents a unique opportunity for participants to contribute to a high-impact project focused on mapping politically exposed and politically influential individuals ahead of South Africa’s municipal elections scheduled for November 2026. This fellowship is part of a collaborative initiative between Code for Africa and the Norwegian Ministry of Foreign Affairs, designed to strengthen information integrity and improve transparency in public governance through open civic data systems. About the Civic Graph Fellowship The Civic Graph Fellowship is a three-month, part-time programme that focuses on collecting, verifying, and organizing civic data related to Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) and Politically Influential Persons (PIPs) in South Africa. As elections approach, the need for accurate, accessible, and verified civic information becomes increasingly important. The fellowship seeks to address this need by building comprehensive profiles of political actors using open-source data collection methodologies and standardized civic data formats. Selected fellows will work closely with Code for Africa’s iLab team to gather, validate, and organize critical information that contributes to transparency, accountability, and democratic participation. The project aims to create interoperable civic datasets that can be used by journalists, researchers, civil society organizations, fact-checkers, and citizens interested in understanding the political landscape. What Fellows Will Do Participants selected for the programme will undertake a variety of research and data collection activities designed to build reliable civic information systems. Key responsibilities include: Identifying Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) and Politically Influential Persons (PIPs) Collecting and verifying publicly available civic information Gathering official social media profiles and digital identifiers Researching political figures using verified public records Maintaining structured civic databases Applying quality assurance measures to validate collected information Collaborating with the Code for Africa iLab research team The primary sources used for identifying political actors will include: Electoral commission datasets Government gazettes Public registries Official government publications Verified institutional databases Fellows will also collect publicly available information such as: Gender data Official Facebook pages Verified X (formerly Twitter) accounts Instagram profiles Campaign websites Personal websites Wikipedia profiles Other public digital identifiers Importance of Data Verification One of the most critical aspects of the fellowship is ensuring the accuracy of collected information. Participants will be expected to verify that: Social media accounts belong to the correct individual URLs point to official or authentic channels Verification badges are present where applicable Profile details match known public biographies Public records support the identity information collected These verification procedures help prevent misinformation, impersonation, and misidentification while improving the reliability of civic datasets. Working with the Troll Tracker Platform A key component of the initiative involves integrating verified political profiles into the Troll Tracker platform. Troll Tracker uses advanced technologies such as: Machine Learning (ML) Natural Language Processing (NLP) The platform monitors public communication by political actors and helps identify trends in messaging over time. It can also flag deleted posts and detect potentially harmful content from monitored accounts. By combining civic data with digital monitoring systems, the project contributes to stronger information integrity and accountability during election periods. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive several benefits throughout the programme. Monthly Stipend Participants will receive a competitive monthly stipend during the fellowship period. Mentorship Fellows will work under the guidance of experienced professionals from Code for Africa’s iLab team, gaining practical experience in civic technology, open-source intelligence, and data verification. Hands-On Experience The programme provides real-world experience in: Civic technology Open-source intelligence (OSINT) Political data research Information verification Data management Election integrity initiatives Professional Networking Participants will gain exposure to one of Africa’s largest civic technology organizations and collaborate with professionals working in democracy, governance, media, and technology sectors. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should demonstrate: Strong working knowledge of Google Sheets and spreadsheet formulas Fluency in English Fluency in at least one of the following languages: Afrikaans isiXhosa isiZulu Sepedi Sesotho Setswana Experience collecting data from social media platforms including TikTok, Facebook, X, YouTube, and Telegram Ability to follow open-source data collection standards Strong communication and teamwork skills Applicants who are analysts, technologists, or university students with civic data experience are particularly encouraged to apply. About Code for Africa Code for Africa is Africa’s largest civic technology and open-data incubator. The organization supports digital democracy initiatives, investigative journalism projects, and open-data ecosystems that empower citizens with actionable information. Through innovative technology solutions, data-driven projects, and civic engagement initiatives, Code for Africa continues to strengthen accountability, transparency, and public participation across the continent. Final Thoughts The Code for Africa Civic Graph Fellowship 2026 offers a remarkable opportunity for South African researchers, students, analysts, and civic technology enthusiasts to contribute to democratic accountability while developing highly valuable data and research skills. By participating in this fellowship, successful applicants will gain practical experience in civic data collection, open-source intelligence, information verification, and election transparency initiatives while working alongside one of Africa’s leading civic technology organizations. For individuals passionate about democracy, data transparency, civic engagement, and public-interest technology, this fellowship represents an exceptional platform for professional growth and meaningful impact. Deadline : 28th June, 2026 APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here. title: Claude Corps Fellowship 2026: Anthropic Launches $150 Million Initiative to Train and Employ 1,000 AI Fellows Across U.S. Nonprofits
summary: Artificial intelligence is transforming industries, workplaces, and communities around the world. As AI adoption accelerates, organizations are increasingly seeking talent that can help them integrate these technologies effectively and responsibly. To bridge this gap while creating meaningful career opportunities for young professionals, Anthropic has launched the Claude Corps Fellowship 2026, a groundbreaking national initiative designed to place AI-trained fellows inside nonprofit organizations across the United States. Backed by an initial investment of $150 million, Claude Corps aims to train, mentor, and employ 1,000 early-career professionals who will spend a year helping nonprofits leverage AI tools and systems to advance their missions. The program represents one of the largest AI-focused fellowship initiatives ever announced and seeks to ensure that the benefits of artificial intelligence are distributed more broadly across society. What is the Claude Corps Fellowship? Claude Corps is a national fellowship program established by Anthropic, the company behind Claude AI. The initiative is built on the belief that while AI has tremendous potential to create positive change, it also brings economic and workforce disruptions that must be addressed responsibly. Through the fellowship, Anthropic will recruit and train 1,000 fellows, equipping them with advanced AI skills and practical experience using Claude. These fellows will then be matched with nonprofit organizations throughout the United States, where they will work full-time for twelve months to develop AI-powered tools, automate processes, improve operational efficiency, and help organizations maximize their impact. The fellowship is a collaborative effort between three major organizations: Anthropic, which funds the program, provides strategic direction, and offers AI expertise. CodePath, which serves as the employer of record, recruits fellows, and delivers training and mentorship. Social Finance, which oversees program evaluation and long-term scaling efforts. Fellowship Structure and Duration Each Claude Corps fellowship lasts for 12 months and is designed as a full-time, in-person experience. Fellows begin their journey with intensive training focused on applying AI tools within nonprofit environments. After placement with host organizations, fellows continue receiving approximately five hours of weekly professional development and AI training while spending the majority of their time supporting the nonprofit’s projects and strategic objectives. This structure ensures that participants continue developing their technical skills while gaining valuable workplace experience. Salary and Benefits One of the most attractive features of the Claude Corps Fellowship is its comprehensive compensation package. Selected fellows will receive: Annual salary of $85,000 Comprehensive employee benefits Full-time employment through CodePath Access to extensive Claude AI resources and token budgets Ongoing mentorship and coaching Technical support from Anthropic experts Professional development opportunities Access to a national network of fellows and peers These benefits make the fellowship highly competitive among early-career opportunities in the AI and nonprofit sectors. Organizations Hosting Fellows Anthropic plans to place fellows in more than 400 nonprofit organizations across the United States. These organizations span diverse sectors, including education, social services, environmental conservation, refugee support, public policy, arts and culture, mental health, and community development. Examples of participating organizations include Code for America, Goodwill Industries International, International Rescue Committee (IRC), RAINN, Water For People, YMCA chapters, museums, and numerous community-focused nonprofits. By embedding fellows directly within these organizations, Claude Corps aims to create practical AI solutions that strengthen nonprofit operations while simultaneously providing fellows with hands-on experience solving real-world challenges. Why Claude Corps Matters As AI continues reshaping the future of work, organizations across all sectors need professionals who understand how to implement AI responsibly and effectively. Claude Corps addresses two critical challenges simultaneously: First, it helps nonprofit organizations access cutting-edge AI expertise that they may otherwise struggle to afford. Second, it creates meaningful employment and career development opportunities for young professionals entering an evolving workforce. Anthropic believes that companies developing transformative technologies have a responsibility to help communities adapt to these changes and ensure that AI’s benefits are widely shared. Claude Corps represents one of the company’s most significant investments toward that goal. How to Apply Applications for the Claude Corps Fellowship are currently open. Interested candidates can submit their applications through the official Claude Corps website. Applicants will undergo a selection process focused on their AI experience, communication abilities, motivation, and commitment to addressing societal challenges. Successful applicants will be matched with host organizations based on project alignment, mutual interest, and geographic considerations. Application Deadline The application deadline for the Claude Corps Fellowship 2026 is July 17, 2026. Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to maximize their chances of consideration. Final Thoughts The Claude Corps Fellowship 2026 is more than just a fellowship—it is a national effort to prepare the next generation of AI leaders while empowering nonprofits to achieve greater impact. With a competitive salary, extensive training, mentorship, and the opportunity to work on meaningful projects, the program offers a unique pathway for early-career professionals who want to combine technology, social impact, and career growth. For aspiring AI practitioners interested in making a difference while developing in-demand skills, Claude Corps stands out as one of the most exciting fellowship opportunities currently available. Deadline : July 17,2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities , click here. title: Fully Funded Bournemouth University PhD Studentship in Movement Ecology and Conservation of Threatened Sea Lamprey and European Eel Populations in Reconnected Rivers
summary: Students seeking a fully funded doctoral research opportunity in ecology, conservation biology, fisheries science, and environmental sustainability now have an exciting chance to pursue advanced research at Bournemouth University in the United Kingdom. Bournemouth University is offering a prestigious PhD Studentship focused on the movement ecology and biology of threatened sea lamprey and European eel populations in reconnected rivers. This highly specialized doctoral research project addresses one of the most pressing environmental challenges facing freshwater ecosystems today: the decline of migratory fish populations caused by habitat fragmentation, river barriers, climate change, and human activities. The project aims to generate new scientific knowledge that will support conservation efforts and help restore sustainable populations of some of Europe’s most threatened aquatic species. About the PhD Studentship Freshwater ecosystems provide essential benefits to society, including drinking water, food production, biodiversity conservation, and renewable energy resources. However, decades of human intervention have severely altered many river systems across Europe. The construction of dams, weirs, and other barriers has interrupted natural migration routes used by fish species for spawning, feeding, and survival. Among the species most affected by these changes are the European eel (Anguilla anguilla) and the sea lamprey (Petromyzon marinus). Both species have experienced significant population declines across Europe and are now considered conservation priorities. Restoring river connectivity through river reconnection projects has become an important strategy for supporting their recovery, yet many questions remain regarding how these species respond to restored habitats and newly accessible migration routes. This PhD project seeks to investigate the movement ecology, migration patterns, habitat use, and biological responses of these threatened species in rivers that have undergone reconnection efforts. The findings will contribute to evidence-based conservation management and inform future river restoration initiatives. Research Significance The project is positioned within Bournemouth University’s strong research tradition in fish ecology and conservation. Researchers within the Fish Ecology and Conservation (FishE) research cluster have developed internationally recognized expertise in understanding fish movements, migration ecology, and freshwater biodiversity conservation. The study will explore critical ecological questions such as: How river reconnection influences migration success. How sea lamprey and European eel populations respond to improved habitat connectivity. The movement behaviour of migratory fish throughout different life stages. Factors affecting survival, reproduction, and habitat selection. The effectiveness of conservation interventions aimed at restoring aquatic biodiversity. The project is expected to produce important scientific evidence that can guide policymakers, environmental agencies, river managers, and conservation organizations across Europe. Research Methods and Training Opportunities Successful candidates will gain extensive training in advanced ecological research techniques. Depending on the project’s final design, students may be involved in: Field surveys and fish monitoring. Fish tagging and telemetry studies. Movement tracking technologies. Biodiversity assessment. Ecological data analysis. Statistical modelling. Conservation planning. Scientific writing and publication. Presentation of research findings at conferences and scientific meetings. The project offers an exceptional opportunity to develop both practical field skills and advanced analytical expertise while contributing to real-world conservation outcomes. Funding Benefits This fully funded PhD Studentship provides comprehensive financial support for the successful candidate. Benefits include: Full tuition fee waiver. Annual maintenance stipend to support living expenses. Funding support for fieldwork activities. Conference attendance support. Research training opportunities. Access to Bournemouth University’s research facilities and expert supervision. The funding package enables students to focus entirely on their research while developing expertise in a highly important area of environmental science. Why Study at Bournemouth University? Bournemouth University has established itself as a leading institution for environmental and ecological research. Through its Doctoral College and specialist research groups, students receive strong academic support, professional development opportunities, and access to collaborative research networks. The university’s Fish Ecology and Conservation research cluster has an established record of conducting impactful research on migratory fish species, freshwater ecosystems, and biodiversity conservation throughout Europe. Students benefit from working alongside experienced researchers who are actively engaged in international conservation initiatives. In addition to academic training, doctoral researchers are encouraged to participate in conferences, workshops, seminars, and collaborative projects that enhance their professional development and future career prospects. Career Opportunities After Completion Graduates from this PhD programme can pursue careers in: Academic research and teaching. Environmental consultancy. Fisheries management. Government environmental agencies. Conservation organizations. International environmental NGOs. River restoration and biodiversity projects. Ecological monitoring and assessment. The specialized knowledge gained through this project will be highly valuable in sectors focused on biodiversity conservation, sustainability, and environmental management. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should demonstrate exceptional academic potential and a strong commitment to completing doctoral-level research. Typically, successful candidates will possess: A First-Class Honours Degree (or equivalent). A Master’s Degree with distinction (or equivalent). Academic background relevant to ecology, environmental sciences, biology, conservation, fisheries science, zoology, or related fields. Strong research aptitude and motivation. English language proficiency requirements where applicable. International students whose first language is not English may be required to demonstrate English language proficiency according to Bournemouth University’s requirements. Application Information Prospective applicants are encouraged to review the full project description and application procedures on Bournemouth University’s official website. Early preparation is highly recommended, including gathering academic transcripts, preparing supporting documents, and developing a clear understanding of the proposed research area. This PhD Studentship represents a unique opportunity for aspiring researchers who wish to contribute to the conservation of threatened aquatic species while obtaining a fully funded doctoral degree from a respected UK university. Deadline: 30 th June ,2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here. title: NIHR Programme Grants for Applied Research (June 2026): Up to Multi-Million Pound Funding Available for Transformative Health and Social Care Research
summary: The National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Programme Grants for Applied Research (PGfAR) funding opportunity for June 2026. This prestigious funding scheme is designed to support ambitious, multidisciplinary programmes of applied health and social care research that can generate significant benefits for patients, service users, carers, and healthcare systems. The Programme Grants for Applied Research scheme is one of NIHR’s flagship funding mechanisms and provides substantial financial support for research programmes that address important health and social care challenges. Unlike smaller project grants, PGfAR funding supports a coordinated programme of interconnected research projects working together under a common theme to produce meaningful and practical improvements in healthcare delivery and outcomes. What is the NIHR Programme Grants for Applied Research Scheme? The Programme Grants for Applied Research (PGfAR) scheme enables research teams to undertake collaborative, multidisciplinary programmes of applied research aimed at solving pressing health and social care challenges. The programme emphasizes research with practical applications and a clear pathway to improving patient outcomes and strengthening health and care services. Funded programmes typically consist of several interrelated work packages and projects that collectively address a coherent research question or theme. These programmes often involve expertise from multiple disciplines, including: Clinical research Public health Health economics Biostatistics Behavioural sciences Qualitative research Social care research Patient and public involvement specialists The multidisciplinary nature of the programme ensures that funded research can comprehensively address complex healthcare challenges and generate evidence that can be translated into practice. Funding Scope and Value One of the most attractive aspects of the PGfAR programme is its substantial funding level. The scheme supports large-scale applied health and social care research programmes, with awards typically ranging from approximately £2 million to £3.5 million over a period of four to six years. Exceptional proposals may receive funding tailored to the scale and complexity of the proposed research programme. Funding duration and award size are determined based on the nature of the proposed work, including whether the programme includes major clinical trials or other large-scale research activities. This flexibility allows research teams to design programmes that effectively address their research objectives without being constrained by rigid funding limits. Key Objectives of the Programme The NIHR PGfAR scheme aims to support research that: Improves patient outcomes Enhances health and social care services Addresses major evidence gaps in healthcare Strengthens healthcare decision-making Supports innovation in healthcare delivery Generates practical evidence for policy and practice Improves the efficiency and effectiveness of health systems Research funded under this programme is expected to have clear real-world relevance and demonstrate the potential to produce tangible benefits within a relatively short timeframe compared to basic or laboratory-based research. Who Should Apply? The programme is particularly suited for established research teams with a strong track record of delivering high-quality applied health and social care research. Successful applications often involve partnerships between: Universities NHS organisations Social care providers Public health institutions Community organisations Patient and public representatives Applicants are encouraged to build strong collaborations that bring together complementary expertise and perspectives. Application Process The June 2026 funding opportunity follows a two-stage application process. Stage 1: Outline Application Applicants must first submit an outline proposal that presents the overall research programme, objectives, methodology, expected outcomes, and potential impact. Stage 2: Full Application Research teams whose outline applications are shortlisted will be invited to submit a detailed full application. This stage requires comprehensive information about research design, work packages, budgets, timelines, governance arrangements, and impact strategies. Why This Opportunity Matters Healthcare systems globally continue to face complex challenges, including increasing patient demand, workforce pressures, chronic disease management, health inequalities, and the need for evidence-based innovation. The NIHR PGfAR scheme plays a critical role in generating the evidence required to address these issues effectively. Funded programmes frequently influence clinical practice, healthcare policy, service design, and patient care pathways. Through its emphasis on applied research, the programme ensures that funded studies move beyond academic publication and contribute directly to healthcare improvements. Benefits of the Programme Successful applicants can benefit from: Multi-million-pound research funding Support for large-scale collaborative research Opportunities to influence healthcare policy and practice Enhanced institutional research capacity Strong patient and public involvement structures National recognition through NIHR funding Long-term impact on health and social care delivery The programme also contributes to building research leadership capacity and fostering enduring partnerships across healthcare, academia, and policy sectors. Deadline 5th August,2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here. The post NIHR Programme Grants for Applied Research (June 2026): Up to Multi-Million Pound Funding Available for Transformative Health and Social Care Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: PMAFF Perfoming Arts Grant 2026: Up to $250000 Funding Opportunity For Chicago Arts Organizations to Create Bold New Performance Works
summary: The Paul M. Angell Family Foundation (PMAFF) has launched a transformative Request for Proposals (RFP) titled “Envisioning the Future: Holistic Resources for New Work Development.” This initiative is designed to strengthen Chicago’s performing arts ecosystem by funding the creation and development of original, bold, and culturally relevant performance works. Through this pilot grant program, PMAFF aims to support organizations that are pushing artistic boundaries in theatre, dance, music, and multidisciplinary performance. The foundation’s focus is not only on artistic excellence but also on community impact, empathy-building, and the reimagining of shared futures through the arts. This opportunity is particularly significant because it expands funding beyond traditional theatre and classical music to include a wider range of performance genres. It also represents PMAFF’s commitment to deepening relationships with new and recently unfunded organizations. Purpose and Vision of the Grant Program The PMAFF Performing Arts Program recognizes the arts as a powerful force for social connection, emotional expression, and cultural transformation. Through this funding initiative, the foundation seeks to: Support new works that respond to contemporary societal challenges Encourage artists and organizations to envision inclusive and hopeful futures Strengthen communities through shared artistic experiences Promote empathy, healing, and cultural exchange Amplify underrepresented voices in the performing arts This initiative is structured to ensure that supported projects move beyond concept development into fully realized performances within a three-year timeline. Funding Structure and Phases This grant program is structured in two major phases: Phase One: Development and Creation Supports administrative and artistic costs related to developing new work Up to 30 organizations will be selected for further review and site visits Final selection of up to 25 organizations will receive funding Grants of up to $150,000 may be awarded depending on project scope and organizational capacity Phase Two: Production and Expansion (2027 Cycle) Organizations selected in Phase One will be invited to apply for renewal funding This phase supports full production and marketing of completed works Grants of up to $250,000 may be awarded All awarded funds must be fully utilized within three years of the first payment. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for this opportunity, applicants must meet strict eligibility criteria. Eligible organizations must be: U.S.-based nonprofit organizations with 501(c)(3) status Arts-focused organizations where performing arts is the primary mission Located in Chicago or surrounding suburbs (Cook County) Operating continuously since January 1, 2023 Organizations with annual budgets of up to $10 million Groups producing original performance work (theatre, dance, music, or multidisciplinary forms) Organizations proposing new, original works (not adaptations or already-developed productions) Non-Eligible Applicants The following are not eligible: For-profit or commercial organizations Individual artists or fiscal sponsor applications Organizations funded by PMAFF Performing Arts Program since January 1, 2023 Organizations outside Chicago/Cook County Projects that adapt existing works Projects already in production stage Grant Selection Criteria Applications will be assessed based on: Alignment with eligibility requirements Artistic relevance to contemporary issues and future visions Contribution to equity, inclusion, and cultural representation Organizational capacity and project feasibility Artistic excellence and prior performance history Fair and equitable compensation for artists Audience engagement and accessibility strategies Feasibility of completing development and production within three years Application Requirements Applicants must submit responses addressing: Organizational mission and background Description of the proposed new performance work Artistic and community engagement goals Equity and inclusion practices Artist involvement and collaboration history Organizational readiness and production capacity Project timeline and development plan Required attachments include: Organizational budget Project budget Audit or Form 990 Board list Donor list Artist bios and work samples Applications must be submitted through the PMAFF grant portal. Timeline and Key Dates RFP Launch: June 8, 2026 Informational Webinar: June 16, 2026 (12:00–1:00 PM CT) Office Hours: Weekly (15-minute slots available) Application Deadline: July 31, 2026 at 5:00 PM CT Round 1 Selection: Early August 2026 Site Visits and Review: August 2026 Final Decisions: November 2026 Phase 2 Applications Open: 2027 Why This Opportunity Matters This grant program represents more than funding; it is an investment in the future of performing arts. By supporting organizations that create original works, PMAFF is fostering innovation, cultural dialogue, and artistic leadership in Chicago’s creative ecosystem. The initiative also emphasizes accessibility, ensuring that communities can experience new performances at low or no cost, thereby expanding cultural participation and audience development. For organizations ready to take bold creative risks and engage deeply with community storytelling, this opportunity offers both financial support and long-term developmental partnership. Conclusion The PMAFF “Envisioning the Future” RFP is a rare and highly competitive funding opportunity for Chicago-based performing arts organizations. With significant financial support, a structured development pathway, and a strong emphasis on equity and innovation, this grant is positioned to shape the next generation of performance art in the United States. Organizations with strong artistic vision, community engagement strategies, and production capacity are strongly encouraged to apply before the July 31, 2026 deadline. Deadline : July ,31 2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity , click here. For more global opportunities , click here. title: Communities Climate Adaptation Facility (CCAF) for Belize 2026: UNOPS and Global Centre for Climate Mobility Launch Funding Opportunity for Community-Led Climate Resilience Projects
summary: Climate change continues to threaten vulnerable communities around the world, particularly those facing displacement, environmental degradation, loss of livelihoods, and limited access to essential services. To address these challenges, the United Nations Office for Project Services (UNOPS), through the Global Centre for Climate Mobility (GCCM), has launched the Communities Climate Adaptation Facility (CCAF) for Belize 2026 . This grant opportunity is designed to support community-driven climate adaptation initiatives that strengthen resilience, improve livelihoods, protect vulnerable populations, and help communities adapt to climate-related impacts. Organizations working in Belize now have the opportunity to secure funding for projects that create sustainable and inclusive climate solutions. About the Communities Climate Adaptation Facility (CCAF) The Communities Climate Adaptation Facility (CCAF) is a grant funding initiative focused on helping communities most affected by climate risks. Through this call for proposals, UNOPS and the Global Centre for Climate Mobility aim to support interventions that promote climate resilience while ensuring social inclusion and sustainable development. The program recognizes that climate change affects communities differently, particularly those already experiencing social and economic vulnerabilities. Therefore, the facility prioritizes projects that deliver practical and measurable adaptation outcomes while strengthening local economies and social systems. Reference Information Programme: Communities Climate Adaptation Facility (CCAF) Implementing Organization: UNOPS Supported By: Global Centre for Climate Mobility (GCCM) Reference Number: BZ/GCCM/24498/2026/001 Beneficiary Country: Belize Published Date: 8 June 2026 Application Deadline: 26 June 2026, 23:00 (GMT -5) Objectives of the Grant Program The CCAF grant program seeks to support initiatives that address the growing impacts of climate change while promoting sustainable development. The program aims to: Strengthen community resilience to climate-related shocks. Improve access to essential services for vulnerable populations. Support climate adaptation and integration of relocated populations. Promote sustainable livelihoods and economic opportunities. Restore and protect ecosystems affected by climate change. Advance inclusive and equitable climate adaptation solutions. Support vulnerable groups through community-centered interventions. What Types of Projects Can Be Supported? The CCAF community grants support projects across multiple sectors that contribute to climate adaptation and resilience. Eligible project areas may include: 1. Water and Sanitation Projects that improve access to safe water and sanitation services for climate-vulnerable communities, particularly in areas affected by droughts, flooding, or environmental degradation. 2. Environmental Protection and Restoration Initiatives focused on restoring ecosystems, protecting natural resources, and enhancing environmental sustainability to strengthen climate resilience. 3. Health and Community Well-Being Programs that address climate-related health risks and improve access to healthcare services for vulnerable populations. 4. Housing and Climate-Resilient Infrastructure Projects that support safe, resilient housing solutions and community infrastructure capable of withstanding climate impacts. 5. Workforce Development and Livelihoods Activities that create economic opportunities, enhance workforce skills, and strengthen livelihood resilience in communities affected by climate change. 6. Access to Government Services Projects that help climate-affected populations access public services and social protection systems. Priority Beneficiaries The CCAF specifically targets communities facing significant climate challenges, including: Communities exposed to climate risks and environmental hazards. Populations isolated due to climate-related impacts. Communities that have lost access to government services because of climate change. Individuals and families displaced or relocating due to climate impacts. Host communities receiving climate-displaced populations. Communities experiencing economic disruptions caused by climate change. Strong Focus on Inclusion and Equity One of the defining features of the Communities Climate Adaptation Facility is its commitment to ensuring that climate adaptation efforts are inclusive and equitable. Special consideration will be given to projects that are: Led by women. Led by Indigenous communities. Led by persons with disabilities. Designed to support marginalized populations. Gender-sensitive and socially inclusive. Culturally appropriate and community-centered. Applicants are encouraged to demonstrate how their proposed activities will contribute to equitable adaptation outcomes and meaningful participation of vulnerable groups. Supporting Climate Mobility and Community Integration Climate mobility is becoming an increasingly important global issue as communities face displacement due to rising sea levels, extreme weather events, and environmental degradation. The CCAF recognizes the importance of supporting both relocated populations and host communities by: Facilitating successful adaptation and integration. Strengthening social cohesion. Expanding livelihood opportunities. Enhancing community resilience. Promoting inclusive local economic development. Projects that address climate mobility challenges while fostering long-term sustainability are particularly relevant under this funding opportunity. Expected Impact Successful projects are expected to contribute to: Increased climate resilience. Improved living conditions. Enhanced community adaptation capacity. Stronger local economies. Greater social inclusion. Improved environmental sustainability. Better livelihood opportunities for vulnerable populations. Preservation of community culture and heritage. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted no later than: 26 June 2026 at 23:00 (GMT -5) Late submissions may not be considered. How to Apply Interested organizations should carefully review the call for proposals and accompanying application documents available through the United Nations Global Marketplace (UNGM) portal. Applicants should prepare all required proposal materials and supporting documents before submission. Apply Here Why Organizations Should Apply This funding opportunity provides organizations in Belize with a unique chance to: Access international climate adaptation funding. Support vulnerable communities. Strengthen local resilience to climate change. Promote sustainable livelihoods. Restore ecosystems and natural resources. Advance inclusive development. Contribute to national and community climate adaptation goals. Develop innovative solutions for climate mobility and displacement challenges. Organizations with strong community engagement, climate adaptation expertise, and a commitment to inclusive development are encouraged to apply before the deadline. For more information on this grant visit here For more grant opportunities on OFY click here The post Communities Climate Adaptation Facility (CCAF) for Belize 2026: UNOPS and Global Centre for Climate Mobility Launch Funding Opportunity for Community-Led Climate Resilience Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ViiV Healthcare Positive Action Funding Opportunities: Up to £300,000 Available for Community-Led HIV and AIDS Initiatives Worldwide
summary: Organizations working to eliminate AIDS and improve the lives of people living with HIV have an opportunity to access significant funding through the ViiV Healthcare Positive Action Programme. The initiative supports community-led and community-based organizations that are developing innovative solutions and scaling proven interventions that address HIV prevention, testing, treatment, care, and long-term support. Established as part of ViiV Healthcare’s commitment to ending HIV and ensuring that no person living with HIV is left behind, Positive Action has become one of the world s leading funding mechanisms for community organizations responding to HIV and AIDS. Over the years, the program has supported hundreds of organizations and projects across more than 100 countries, helping communities address HIV-related challenges through locally led solutions. About the Positive Action Programme Positive Action was created to strengthen the HIV response by investing directly in community organizations that work with populations most affected by HIV. The programme prioritizes community leadership, local ownership, and sustainable solutions that can create long-term impact. The initiative supports organizations that: Improve HIV prevention services. Increase HIV testing and diagnosis. Enhance access to treatment and care. Address HIV-related stigma and discrimination. Support vulnerable and marginalized populations. Strengthen community systems and local leadership. Improve health outcomes for people living with HIV. Funding Streams Available The Positive Action Programme currently offers two primary funding streams designed to support organizations at different stages of program development. 1. Innovator Funding Innovator Funding is intended for organizations seeking to test and pilot new approaches to HIV-related challenges. Funding Available: Up to £100,000 over two years. Maximum of £50,000 per year. Key Features: Supports testing and piloting of innovative approaches. The proposed intervention must be new to the applicant organization. The approach may already have been implemented successfully elsewhere. Designed to generate evidence before a larger-scale rollout. Focuses on experimentation, learning, and innovation. 2. Momentum Funding Momentum Funding supports organizations that already have evidence-based interventions and want to expand or scale them. Funding Available: Up to £300,000 over three years. Maximum of £100,000 per year. Key Features: Supports expansion of proven HIV interventions. Applicants must demonstrate previous implementation experience. Organizations must provide evidence that the intervention works. Funding can build on previous investments from Positive Action or other donors. Emphasizes sustainability beyond the grant period. What Types of Projects Are Supported? Positive Action seeks projects that create measurable impact within communities affected by HIV and AIDS. Examples of supported interventions include: HIV prevention programs. HIV testing and diagnosis initiatives. Linkage-to-care projects. Treatment adherence support. Community health worker programs. HIV stigma reduction campaigns. Mental health support services. Programs supporting women and girls. Youth-focused HIV interventions. Services for marginalized and vulnerable populations. Community-led advocacy initiatives. Capacity-building programs for local organizations. Recent funding rounds have focused on: Paediatric HIV. Adolescent HIV prevention and care. Caregiver support programs. HIV testing and treatment access. Community-led health interventions. Scaling evidence-informed HIV solutions. Why This Funding Matters Community organizations often understand local challenges better than large institutions. Positive Action recognizes that sustainable progress against HIV requires solutions that are designed and led by affected communities themselves. The programme therefore prioritizes: Community leadership. Local ownership. Equity in partnerships. Sustainable development. Capacity strengthening. Long-term impact. Beyond financial support, Positive Action also offers opportunities for organizational development, learning exchanges, networking, and access to technical expertise that can help grantees strengthen their impact and sustainability. Additional Support Available Organizations funded through Positive Action may benefit from: Organizational development support. Learning and networking opportunities. Access to skilled volunteers. Knowledge-sharing platforms. Community programming guidance. Strategic partnerships. Application Process Applications are submitted through the Positive Action online grants portal . Organizations interested in future funding opportunities are encouraged to monitor the official funding opportunities page regularly for announcements regarding new funding rounds and application windows. Deadline As of May 2026, the official Positive Action Funding Opportunities page indicates that there are currently no open funding rounds. Interested organizations should continue checking the official website for upcoming calls for proposals and application deadlines. Final Thoughts The ViiV Healthcare Positive Action Programme remains one of the most impactful global funding initiatives dedicated to supporting community-led responses to HIV and AIDS. With grants of up to £300,000 available through its Momentum Funding stream and up to £100,000 through Innovator Funding, the programme offers a valuable opportunity for organizations seeking to create sustainable, community-driven change. Whether your organization is piloting a new idea or scaling a proven intervention, Positive Action provides both financial resources and strategic support to help communities move closer to the goal of ending AIDS and improving outcomes for people living with HIV worldwide. title: School Enterprise Challenge 2026: Free Global Entrepreneurship Competition for Schools with International Recognition, Prizes, and Real Business Experience
summary: Schools around the world are being invited to participate in the School Enterprise Challenge 2026, a free international entrepreneurship programme that equips students with practical business skills by helping them create, launch, and manage real school-based businesses. Designed to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world experience, the programme has become one of the world s leading school entrepreneurship initiatives, empowering hundreds of thousands of young people globally. The School Enterprise Challenge is organized by Teach A Man To Fish and is open to schools worldwide, from pre-primary to secondary level. Through a structured, step-by-step learning approach, students gain hands-on experience in entrepreneurship while developing critical life and leadership skills that prepare them for future careers and business opportunities. What is the School Enterprise Challenge? The School Enterprise Challenge is a free international education and entrepreneurship programme that supports teachers and students in establishing sustainable school businesses. Rather than learning entrepreneurship through theory alone, students actively participate in developing business ideas, creating business plans, launching enterprises, managing finances, marketing products or services, and evaluating business performance. Since its launch in 2011, the programme has benefited hundreds of thousands of young people worldwide and has become a recognized platform for promoting entrepreneurial thinking among school-aged learners. Schools from numerous countries have participated, creating enterprises ranging from agricultural projects and food production businesses to crafts, technology ventures, and environmental enterprises. Why the School Enterprise Challenge Matters Traditional education often focuses on academic knowledge while providing limited exposure to practical business and entrepreneurship skills. The School Enterprise Challenge addresses this gap by introducing experiential learning opportunities that allow students to learn through action. The programme helps learners develop: Entrepreneurial skills Financial literacy Leadership abilities Teamwork and collaboration Problem-solving skills Creativity and innovation Communication skills Business planning capabilities Decision-making skills Confidence and resilience Students are encouraged to identify opportunities within their communities, design solutions, and create sustainable business models that generate real income while creating positive social impact. Benefits for Students Participating students receive a unique opportunity to experience entrepreneurship firsthand. Key benefits include: 1. Real Business Experience Students do not simply study entrepreneurship; they operate actual businesses. This practical exposure enables them to understand how businesses function in real-world environments. 2. Development of Future-Ready Skills The programme focuses on essential 21st-century competencies that employers and universities increasingly value. These include: Leadership Communication Critical thinking Innovation Collaboration Financial management Project management Strategic planning 3. Improved Academic Performance Research conducted on programme participants has shown positive educational outcomes. Students who participated were found to be significantly more likely to achieve top grades in mathematics and science compared to non-participants. 4. International Exposure Students become part of a global network of entrepreneurial schools and learners, allowing them to exchange ideas and learn from successful school businesses operating in different countries. Benefits for Teachers The School Enterprise Challenge is not only beneficial to students but also provides valuable professional development opportunities for educators. Teachers gain: Access to entrepreneurship education resources. Ready-to-use teaching materials. Practical business education guides. Experiential learning methodologies. Membership in an international educator network. Opportunities to strengthen classroom engagement. Exposure to innovative teaching techniques. The programme enables teachers to bring classroom concepts to life through practical application and student-led learning experiences. Benefits for Schools Participating schools can benefit in multiple ways beyond educational outcomes. Schools may: Generate additional income through student-run businesses. Support school improvement projects. Enhance entrepreneurship education. Strengthen community engagement. Improve student retention and participation. Build a culture of innovation and creativity. Increase opportunities for practical learning. Many schools have successfully reinvested profits from their businesses into educational resources, infrastructure improvements, and community development projects. How the Programme Works The School Enterprise Challenge follows a structured curriculum that guides schools through every stage of the business development process. Participants learn how to: Identify business opportunities. Conduct market research. Generate business ideas. Develop business plans. Create financial projections. Launch school businesses. Manage operations. Monitor performance. Prepare annual reports. Scale successful enterprises. The programme s comprehensive framework helps students understand every aspect of entrepreneurship from idea generation to implementation. Global Impact of the School Enterprise Challenge The programme has demonstrated remarkable success across diverse educational settings worldwide. Examples of successful student-led enterprises include: Agricultural ventures. Food production businesses. School shops. Environmental projects. Community service enterprises. Manufacturing initiatives. Technology-based ventures. Many participating schools have reported increased student engagement, improved learning outcomes, and stronger entrepreneurial mindsets among learners. Registration and Participation The School Enterprise Challenge 2026 is now open for registration . Schools can join free of charge and gain access to the online platform, educational resources, training materials, and guidance needed to develop successful student-led businesses. The programme is available in both English and Spanish, making it accessible to a broad international audience. Deadline The School Enterprise Challenge allows schools to register throughout the year. Specific submission dates for business ideas, business plans, and annual reports may vary depending on the competition cycle. Interested schools are encouraged to register early and regularly check the official website for updates regarding competition timelines and award deadlines. Final Thoughts The School Enterprise Challenge 2026 represents an outstanding opportunity for schools to introduce entrepreneurship into education through practical, hands-on learning. By enabling students to build and operate real businesses, the programme equips young people with the knowledge, confidence, and skills needed to thrive in an increasingly competitive and innovation-driven world. For schools seeking to inspire the next generation of entrepreneurs, leaders, and problem-solvers, the School Enterprise Challenge offers a proven, globally recognized platform that combines education, entrepreneurship, and community impact into one transformative experience.
